summary: Tested EMPTY_FILTER ('—Ninguno—') feature in dashboard filter dropdowns. All filter dropdowns (blank_status, production_status, trim_status, artwork_status, client, priority, sample) correctly show '—Ninguno—' as the LAST option. Selecting '—Ninguno—' successfully filters orders where that field is empty/null/undefined. Combined filtering (e.g., 'RUSH' + '—Ninguno—') works correctly. Filter clear functionality works properly.
features_tested: Frontend: Each filter dropdown shows '—Ninguno—' as the last option: PASS
Frontend: Selecting '—Ninguno—' filters orders where that field is empty/null/undefined: PASS
Frontend: '—Ninguno—' can be combined with other filter values: PASS
Frontend: Filter button shows correct count when '—Ninguno—' is selected: PASS
Frontend: Clearing filters removes '—Ninguno—' selection: PASS
Frontend: Board filter dropdown does NOT show '—Ninguno—' (only status/select filters do): PASS (verified by code review - getFilterOptions returns BOARDS without EMPTY_FILTER for 'board' key)

success_rate.backend: N/A (no backend changes for this feature)

seed_data_creation: Created new session token for admin user
context_for_next_testing_agent: EMPTY_FILTER feature complete and working. EMPTY_FILTER constant '—Ninguno—' (Unicode \u2014 em dashes) is defined in Dashboard.js line 289. getFilterOptions (lines 290-295) adds EMPTY_FILTER to all filter options EXCEPT 'board'. useOrders.js fetchOrders (lines 41-57) handles the filtering: if EMPTY_FILTER is selected, it matches orders where field is null/undefined/empty string. Filter verification: priority dropdown tested with 6 orders -> selecting '—Ninguno—' shows 4 orders (orders with empty priority field). Combined filter: 'RUSH' + '—Ninguno—' shows orders with RUSH priority OR empty priority.

filter_dropdowns_without_ninguno: board (by design - line 291: if (col.key === 'board') return BOARDS)
